---
title: "soybean"
author: "Durga Chinthalapudi"
date: "2024-07-17"
output: html_document
---
```{r}

mpm = readxl::read_excel("MPM_combined.xlsx")

# Assuming 'mpm' is your data frame
mpm$Date <- as.factor(mpm$Date)  # Convert Date to factor
# Convert Nitrogen and Sulphur to factors
mpm$Nitrogen <- as.factor(mpm$Nitrogen)
mpm$Sulphur <- as.factor(mpm$Sulphur)

str(mpm)

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# Assuming 'mpm' is your data frame
mpm$Date <- as.Date(mpm$Date)

# Pivoting the data into long format
mpm_long <- mpm %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ChlM, FlvM, AnthM, NFI), names_to = "Response", values_to = "Value")

plot = ggplot(mpm_long, aes(x = Date, y = Value, color = Response)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of Response Variables Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free_y")

plot
```

```{r}
plot2 = # Create the scatter plot with regression lines to assess linear relationships
ggplot(mpm_long, aes(x = Date, y = Value, color = Response)) +
  geom_point() +  # Scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +  # Linear regression lines
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of Response Variables Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 week", date_labels = "%b %d") # Customize x-axis for better readability

plot2
```

```{r}
nitrogen = # Create the box plot
ggplot(mpm, aes(x = Nitrogen, y = ChlM, fill = Nitrogen)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Effect of Nitrogen and Sulphur on Pigments",
       x = "Nitrogen and Sulphur Interaction",
       y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

nitrogen
# Save the plot (optional)
```



```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
